title: Segfault with `StringIO.__getstate__` of an instance in invalid state
author: devdanzin
body:
It's possible to segfault PyPy by calling `__getstate__`, `getvalue`, `read` etc.  on an instance of `StringIO` that is in an invalid state:

```python
from io import StringIO

obj = StringIO("\x00")
obj.writelines("\x00")
try:
    obj.__init__(1j)
except TypeError:
    pass
obj.__getstate__()
```

`pypy -VV`:
```
Python 3.10.14 (5cccf8d934d7, Nov 17 2024, 04:47:11)
[PyPy 7.3.18-alpha0 with GCC 10.2.1 20210130 (Red Hat 10.2.1-11)]
```

Found using fusil by @vstinner.
